programme.title: The Bill

Desperate for a chance to redeem herself, she makes one last effort to gather incriminating evidence against Gabriel - only to end up being held hostage in a store room by the crazed copper. Events come to a head when Colin is dismissed after being exposed as a vicious racist and retaliates by handcuffing Ken in a petrol-doused van and crashing it into Sun Hill's front office. Blissfully unaware of the escalating crisis, Reg calls Marilyn's mobile phone at the station, intending to ask for her hand in marriage - and triggers a massive explosion(n)

programme.title: The Bill

Dale also faces a fiery demise in the burning building, while Jim struggles to accept the news that his friend Ken was killed instantly when the van blew up. Marilyn fights for life in hospital, a grief-stricken Reg at her bedside - and as he tearfully slips an engagement ring onto her finger, an ominous bleep from her cardiac monitor heralds a tragic end to their relationship(n)

programme.desc: The town's residents prepare for Lady Ludlow's garden party, but the occasion is marred by rumours the railway line will come right into Cranford, leaving Deborah feeling betrayed. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Christmas arrives in the town, but Lady Ludlow regrets any notion of seasonal charity when Mr Carter asks her to intervene in the case of Job Gregson, who has been blamed for a spate of thefts. Miss Matty is delighted when Mr Holbrook invites her to his farm, and Dr Harrison's friend Jack sends Caroline Tomkinson a card hinting she should expect a proposal from the medic. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Philip Glenister(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. Penultimate episode of the costume drama, starring Judi Dench and Simon Woods(n)

Also featured are royal letters from Queen Victoria's children, a pawned diamond ring that saved a family from the poorhouse, a rare ninth-century gold coin and some valuable Chinese surprises.(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia's relationship comes under increasing strain, while Ted's arrival from New Zealand raises questions. Raff is left speechless when a giraffe turns up at Far Slack Farm and Gillian is riddled with old anxieties when it brings unwarranted attention to the barn. Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, as all four teams looked too boost their chances of playing European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Football Stars in Bad Cars
programme.desc: Fulham team-mates Tom Cairney, Steven Sessegnon and Marcus Bettinelli drive a £2,000 hatchback for the day and play games set by BBC Radio 1's Chris Stark via the car radio. The players then pass the car on to Norwich City after giving it a makeover so that it is even more embarrassing to drive(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores Worm's Head on the Gower Peninsula, and learns about the quarrying heritage of Abereiddy and Porthgain, while Alice Roberts discovers the musical properties of the beach on Porth Oer(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a retired couple escape to the tranquillity of Norfolk after 30 years in a busy Essex town. Now that their children have flown the nest, the pair are hoping to find a country home that's full of character for their £350,000 budget. Elsewhere, Margherita discovers Norfolk's answer to the Great Barrier Reef(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present more wildlife reports live from their base in the Cairngorms. They reveal the science behind why ducks don't die of cold, hear about the mini beasts captured on camera by a passionate amateur photographer and share the mass spectacle of 30,000 redwings roosting in a remote wood in Lancashire's Ribble Valley(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and her guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Plus, updates from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: In York, the winners of the best detached, best apartment, best semi-detached and best wildcard property are revisited by the judges for a second inspection. The arbiters must make their ultimate decision and crown only one of them the week's Best House in Town(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale prepares more dishes designed to show that cooking quality food need not be a time-consuming and difficult process. In this episode, she turns her attention to her favourite classic dishes, and demonstrates how to make them both more appetising and easier to prepare(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: A distraught Aoife Burke arrives at the station, furious following Ash and Paudge's failure to inform her of the discovery of her mother's body(n)

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison concludes her exploration of Britain's winter landscapes with a trip to the Shetland archipelago. Here she witnesses the mesmerising beauty of the Northern Lights, encounters Viking practices alongside present-day crofting and learns about seal and otter pup rescues - each of which help prove life is far from dull for the islands' inhabitants during this bleak season(n)

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall heads to the island of Lundy to discover why nearly half the world's population of grey seals choose to live in UK waters. Meanwhile, Lindsey Chapman helps return a stranded seal pup to the sea and wildlife cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones tracks down a group of the common seals living in a surprisingly urban setting. Plus, Matt Baker witnesses an extraordinary gathering of animals in Monterey Bay, America(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, Charles Hanson values a rare Eureka clock and Anita Manning is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley. Host Paul Martin meets up with Michelin-star chef Nigel Haworth to discover how to make the perfect Lancashire hotpot(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo reaches Birmingham and discovers how a radical reformer made his mark on the 'city of a thousand trades'. At Highbury Hall, he finds Joseph Chamberlain's mansion home and hears how he established a political dynasty including a prime minister and a foreign secretary. Next stop is Cradley Heath, once one of five chain-making towns in the West Midlands. At the centre of Britain's ceramic industry in Stoke-on-Trent, Michael visits Wedgwood, established by 'Queen's Potter' Josiah Wedgwood in 1759 and visited in 1913 by King George V and Queen Mary(n)

programme.title: Extreme Wales with Richard Parks
programme.desc: The endurance athlete embarks on three adrenaline-filled journeys that explore the very best of Wales and what it has to offer for the keen adventurer. He begins with a four-day cycle ride from the Brecon Beacons to the summit of Snowdon, meeting a mountain-biking pioneer and receiving a lesson from the world downhill champion along the way(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedians Jamali Maddix and Bobby Mair compete in West Yorkshire, guided by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Angus Ashworth. Between browsing for bargains and battling for the best punchline, Angus and Bobby explore the fascinating history of the printed page around the city of Leeds, while Jamali and Natasha explore how the strange evolution of the optical toy led to the profusion of games and media encountered today. In spite of advice, one funnyman thinks a porcelain parrot will be profitable, while the other invests in a toy robot. The duo then take their purchases to auction in Bourne End, Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the south-western coastline of Scotland, with Mehreen taking to the skies with former RAF fighter pilot Archie Liggat, who is now using his skills to document large-scale pollution hidden along the coastline. Adrian, meanwhiile, visits Portpatrick to meet Calum Currie, who has persuaded his community to buy shares in the town's harbour - revitalising it for all who live there. Plus, Patrick Aryee joins volunteers help to track rare birdlife on the Ailsa Craig and Joe Lindsay visits an artists' colony that was the setting for cult film The Wicker Man(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The last of two compilations of highlights from the 17th series of the quiz with a difference, hosted by Sandi Toksvig. Guests including Tom Allen, Jo Brand, Susan Calman, Alan Carr, Jimmy Carr, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Phill Jupitus, Nish Kumar, Prue Leith, Joe Lycett, Jason Manford, Sarah Millican, David Mitchell, Johnny Vegas, Holly Walsh and Josh Widdicombe joined regular panellist Alan Davies to answer questions based on subjects beginning with the letter Q(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Scottish creativity on a journey from Oban to Glasgow. Travelling through the Highlands, Frankie visits the home of Grant Morrison, who has written stories for some of the comic world's most iconic characters. Resuming his trek, he travels from Dunoon to Bannockburn, the site of Robert the Bruce's most famous battle, where he learns how to fight with a sword and shield just like the Scots Knights of the 1300s. Frankie also witnesses young artists brightening up the darker corners of Glasgow and reflects on the city's shameful past, profiting off slavery in the British colonies(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's important national and international news stories, with Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: The Big Sick (2017)
programme.desc: A struggling stand-up comedian proves a hit with a student heckler, but their affair ends just before a mystery illness puts the object of his affections in a coma. Finding himself absorbed with Emily's care and bonding with her sparring parents, Kumail also has to struggle with his own Muslim relatives and their expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Kumail Nanjiani, Zoe Kazan, Holly Hunter and Ray Romano(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the downfall of Alberto Salazar, who coached Britain's greatest track athlete to Olympic glory. Reporter Mark Daly first exposed doping at Salazar's Nike Oregon Project in 2015, and in this investigation he reveals fresh allegations about the disgraced coach and raises new questions about his relationship with Mo Farah(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Great CyberWar, the Doctor arrives in the far future, intent on protecting the last of the human race from the deadly Cybermen. But in the face of such a relentless enemy, has she put her best friends at risk? What terrors lie hiding in the depths of space, and what is Ko Sharmus? Guest starring Julie Graham, Patrick O'Kane and Steve Toussaint(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood view footage of a hailstone the size of a rugby ball, a wall of ice that engulfed a town, and a frog that can freeze and unfreeze(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall chases sailfish in Mexico's Yucatan Peninsula, and dives on a pristine reef in search of a real-life sea monster(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Food
programme.desc: Chef Angela Hartnett heads to Holland to investigate the future of the burger and find out how a 3D printer could soon become an essential kitchen gadget. Dr Shini Somara gets a cooking lesson from a hi-tech kitchen table, greengrocer Chris Bavin visits America to learn how vegetables can be grown without sunlight, and Dara O Briain experiences a mindreading menu that knows what diners wants before they do(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Machli is one of the world's most famous tigers - the star of five films, with her own Facebook page and more than a million YouTube hits of her attacking a crocodile. Wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson returns to India to find his old friend in this documentary charting the extraordinary milestones in Machli's life as a mother and a hunter(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the south-western coastline of Scotland, with Mehreen taking to the skies with former RAF fighter pilot Archie Liggat, who is now using his skills to document large-scale pollution hidden along the coastline. Adrian, meanwhiile, visits Portpatrick to meet Calum Currie, who has persuaded his community to buy shares in the town's harbour - revitalising it for all who live there. Plus, Patrick Aryee joins volunteers help to track rare birdlife on the Ailsa Craig and Joe Lindsay visits an artists' colony that was the setting for cult film The Wicker Man(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip brings him to northern Greece where he enjoys traditional dishes including veal stiffado and kotopita - which Rick declares to be the best chicken pie he has ever tasted. In the coastal city of Preveza he goes out prawn fishing and follows in the footsteps of poet Lord Byron visiting Messolonghi, a city where seafood is a highly regarded commodity. Rick also rolls up his own sleeves to cook his favourite Greek dishes of stuffed peppers and tomatoes and souvlaki(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale demonstrates how to create sophisticated and eye-catching dishes for dinner parties, and reveals tips and tricks that yield maximum results for minimal effort(n)

programme.title: Live Formula E
programme.desc: The Marrakesh ePrix. Jennie Gow presents live coverage of the fifth round of the campaign from the Circuit International Automobile Moulay El Hassan in Morocco. With analysis from Jamie Chadwick, and commentary by Jack Nicholls and Dario Franchitti(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents action from the first four days at the Velodrom in Berlin. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. The volunteers race to the rescue of a shark attack victim, and two fishermen caught by the tide find themselves stranded in a rising sea. Elsewhere, two young boys are swept out to sea in a rip current(n)

programme.title: 10 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: First-time homeowners Alex and Pete welcome rescue pup Pippa, who comes with an extreme chewing habit, while the Woolf family bring home cockapoo pup Rosso - and as with all dogs, the first thing to contend with is toilet training. Emma Lowe reveals how she is getting on training young Labrador Hunter as a therapy dog for her son Alfie, who has Down's syndrome, while psychic Delia faces problems when her Leonberger Shiva begins to bite and scratch. There's also a final catch-up with all 10 families to find out if puppy ownership has been worth it(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In September 2015 in Monterey Bay California, a 30-ton humpback whale breached and landed on Tom Mustill and a friend as they paddled a sea kayak. Both survived the incident though the near-death experience left documentary-maker Tom wondering if the whale was deliberately trying to hurt them. Here, Tom returns to California to investigate that possibility, meeting others who have survived similar encounters and the experts who know the whales best. What Tom discovers raises far bigger questions, not just about what happened that day but also about man's relationship with whales(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Wilson allows the vicar and the verger to enlist in the Home Guard while Mainwaring is in hospital, but their over-enthusiastic approach to man-management brings the wrath of Warden Hodges down upon the platoon. Comedy, first shown in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Bill Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins a new journey on the tracks of the Ocean line to explore Canada's maritime provinces, en route to Quebec City. Along the way he dons a kilt and sporran to join in the recreation of an 18th-century Scottish Highland regiment and learns of a catastrophic explosion in Halifax harbour in 1917, which killed 2,000 people and left 25,000 homeless(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at some 'Queens', inviting Sarah Millican, David Mitchell and Colin Lane to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Kerry Godliman, star of ITV's Bad Move and Taskmaster on Dave, comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. She warms up the audience before introducing routines by the energetic Suzi Ruffell and South African comedian Loyiso Gola(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the shooting Franklin wants everyone in the family to lie low. They do not know how much he is hiding or the pressure he is under, but Melody suspects the truth(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: As Franklin lies between life and death, he considers the kind of man he has become - and the kind of man he wants to be. If he makes it through, might he be the person to try to bring stability to the troubled streets he helped create?(n)

programme.title: Summertime (2015)
programme.desc: An ambitious farmer's daughter travels to Paris in 1971, where she falls in love with a radical feminist. She finds herself obliged to return to the family farm, and her new lover accompanies her on the journey, but their relationship is greeted with disapproval. Romantic drama, starring Cecile De France and Izia Higelin. In French(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke attempt to transform a stagnant, weed-choked pond into a haven both for its owners and the local wildlife(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedians Jamali Maddix and Bobby Mair compete in West Yorkshire, guided by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Angus Ashworth. Between browsing for bargains and battling for the best punchline, Angus and Bobby explore the fascinating history of the printed page around the city of Leeds, while Jamali and Natasha explore how the strange evolution of the optical toy led to the profusion of games and media encountered today. In spite of advice, one funnyman thinks a porcelain parrot will be profitable, while the other invests in a toy robot. The duo then take their purchases to auction in Bourne End, Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown visit Shaftesbury in Dorset to take part in the town's annual snowdrop festival. Plus, Adam Henson meets a trailblazing vet(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Following gardeners in Orkney, Shetland and the Black Isle as they show how they have created stunning sites right on the very edges of Scotland in difficult conditions(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Jasper Carrott back to Barry Island in south Wales, where the comedian reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1958(n)

programme.title: Scott of the Antarctic (1948)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following explorer Robert Falcon Scott's failed expedition to be the first to reach the South Pole. The extreme conditions, poor decision-making and the pressure of the race to make it to the Pole before their Norwegian rivals proved too much for the British team, resulting in tragedy in 1912. Starring John Mills, Derek Bond, James Robertson Justice, Kenneth More and John Gregson(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of day five at the Velodrom in Berlin. Husband and wife Jason and Laura Kenny boast 10 Olympic titles between them, and are targeting more global success at these championships. Laura is likely to complete a busy week by contesting the Madison, while Jason is vying for a third sprint world title. With eight finals being held over the weekend, plenty of other British and global stars are looking to make their mark as the championships - and Olympic qualifying period - draw to a close. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores unusual and unexplained natural events that occur around the world during winter, enlisting the aid of leading scientists to uncover why these strange events happen. The programme examines unusual weather conditions, including tornadoes made of snow, and animals behaving in unexpected ways, from snowboarding crows to penguins attempting death-defying feats(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin head to Israel, where they discover four extraordinary homes, designed to combat the heat and inspired by rich cultural and historical traditions. Among the properties are Barud House in Jerusalem, made from 50,000 white stone tiles, arranged into a symmetrical pattern that casts shadows across the house all day. The final stop is the village of Musmus and the House of Three Gardens, where Tel Aviv architect Ron Fleisher has designed a house that combines traditional Islamic architecture with modernism(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A family of five works in a corner shop to reveal how such businesses have changed over the course of the past 100 years, aided by presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell. The first episode covers shops from the Victorian era to the First World War. The family must learn to bake their own counter goods, measure loose products like tea, flour and sugar by hand, and make deliveries with a horse and cart. They also receive a visit from Kelly Holmes, who puts them through an Edwardian workout and discover the impact of food shortages on corner shops as the war drew on(n)

programme.title: Russia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The traveller's second leg of his journey across Russia takes him from south-east Siberia to the far south west and the majestic Caucasus Mountains. Along the way he stops off at the oldest lake on earth, witnesses a commemoration of the Second World War in the city of Irkutsk and visits a cafe where patriotic staff and punters perform a daily rendition of the national anthem. He also finds out how the collapse of communism has seen millions of disenchanted Russians join new sects and religions, with one community worshipping a man claiming to be the reincarnation of Christ(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris put emergency vehicles to the test to see if the fire brigade, the police or the ambulance service has the fastest vehicles. Chris pays tribute to rally driver Colin McRae, Paddy puts the new BMW M8 through its paces, and comedian Tom Allen joins the team in the studio(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: The two remaining couples pay a visit to the empty property without Duane and Rena, to get a sense of what their new life in the wilderness will really be like. One pair immediately starts hatching grand plans, but tensions are beginning to emerge for their competitors. Eventually, Duane and Rena arrive and set two final tasks - one couple must cut windows into the loft and the other must set up a new windsock on the airstrip. Finally, in the evening, they are each invited to state their cases over why they want to live here before a winner is announced and Duane and Rena bid farewell to their former home(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests, including Sara Pascoe(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Scottish creativity on a journey from Oban to Glasgow. Travelling through the Highlands, Frankie visits the home of Grant Morrison, who has written stories for some of the comic world's most iconic characters. Resuming his trek, he travels from Dunoon to Bannockburn, the site of Robert the Bruce's most famous battle, where he learns how to fight with a sword and shield just like the Scots Knights of the 1300s. Frankie also witnesses young artists brightening up the darker corners of Glasgow and reflects on the city's shameful past, profiting off slavery in the British colonies(n)

programme.title: Manchester by the Sea (2016)
programme.desc: An irritable loner is forced to return to the coastal town where he grew up following the sudden death of his brother. He is appointed guardian of his teenage nephew, and as he struggles to cope with his newfound responsibilities, he also finds himself confronted with a past tragedy. Drama, starring an Oscar-winning Casey Affleck, with Michelle Williams and Kyle Chandler(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: The Track Cycling World Championships continue at the Velodrom in Berlin, where medals are decided in five events - the men's points race, time-trial and individual pursuit, and the women's sprint and omnium. Perhaps surprisingly given their impressive results in recent years, Great Britain were unable to add to the one gold they had won at this stage last year in Poland when these disciplines also took place, with the Netherlands topping the medal table just ahead of Australia(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first shown on April 13, featuring performances by Cookie Crew with Edwin Starr, Ten City, T'Pau, INXS, Fine Young Cannibals, U2 and BB King, Paul Simpson with Adeva, the Bangles and Yello(n)

programme.title: Eric Burdon: Rock 'n' Roll - Animal
programme.desc: How pop group the Animals were important standard bearers of the 'British Invasion' of America, behind the Beatles but ahead of the Rolling Stones, the Who and the Kinks. Alongside his passion for original American blues, frontman Eric Burdon got together in the late 1960s with the black LA band WAR - itself a political statement in the Black Panther era - and, inspired by Charlie Parker, John Coltrane and Rahsaan Roland Kirk, expanded his musical spectrum with jazz and funk. This documentary reveals how Burdon's creative output has made an important and profoundly authentic contribution to popular culture(n)

programme.title: Guitar, Drum and Bass
programme.desc: The former Police musician explores the drums as the founding instrument of popular modern music. Beats that travelled from Africa, via New Orleans and across the world, are the consistent force behind musical evolution. Stewart plays with some of the most inspiring drummers of the past 50 years, including John Densmore of the Doors, Chad Smith of Red Hot Chili Peppers, Prince's musical director Sheila E, New Order's Stephen Morris and the Foo Fighters' Taylor Hawkins. He also goes dancing in New Orleans, builds his own bass drum pedal and checks out hot new bands on Santa Monica beach(n)

programme.title: Burt Bacharach: A Life in Song
programme.desc: Coverage of a unique concert staged at London's Royal Festival Hall celebrating the music of songwriter and performer Burt Bacharach. Some of his most famous songs are performed by artists including Alfie Boe, Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Shaun Escoffery, Rebecca Ferguson, Justin Hayward, Michael Kiwanuka, Laura Mvula and Joss Stone. Burt himself also performs, accompanied by Josie James and his band, and chats to Michael Grade about the art of songwriting and shares the stories behind some of his best-loved hits(n)

programme.title: Classic Comps
programme.desc: Compilations of performances from the BBC archives, including Ravi Shankar's Raaf Bihag and Dudley Moore playing the Colonel Bogey March. Also featuring appearances by guitarist John Williams, cellist Jacqueline du Pre, trumpeter Wynton Marsalis and violinist Nigel Kennedy on programmes including Parkinson, Later with Jools Holland, The Les Dawson Show, Music at Night and Wogan(n)

programme.title: Eric Burdon: Rock 'n' Roll - Animal
programme.desc: How pop group the Animals were important standard bearers of the 'British Invasion' of America, behind the Beatles but ahead of the Rolling Stones, the Who and the Kinks. Alongside his passion for original American blues, frontman Eric Burdon got together in the late 1960s with the black LA band WAR - itself a political statement in the Black Panther era - and, inspired by Charlie Parker, John Coltrane and Rahsaan Roland Kirk, expanded his musical spectrum with jazz and funk. This documentary reveals how Burdon's creative output has made an important and profoundly authentic contribution to popular culture(n)

programme.title: Guitar, Drum and Bass
programme.desc: The former Police musician explores the drums as the founding instrument of popular modern music. Beats that travelled from Africa, via New Orleans and across the world, are the consistent force behind musical evolution. Stewart plays with some of the most inspiring drummers of the past 50 years, including John Densmore of the Doors, Chad Smith of Red Hot Chili Peppers, Prince's musical director Sheila E, New Order's Stephen Morris and the Foo Fighters' Taylor Hawkins. He also goes dancing in New Orleans, builds his own bass drum pedal and checks out hot new bands on Santa Monica beach(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Bologna in northern Italy, a city so famous for its food that it's known as the stomach of Italy. He learns how to make the local fresh egg pasta used for tortellini, tagliatelle, ravioli and lasagne and enjoys stuffed rabbit, filled with Parma ham and Parmesan frittata for dinner. Later, Rick drives up into the hills to a cheese dairy where he tastes a 13-year aged Parmesan and enjoys lunch in at a pig farm with restaurant attached, where huge charcoal-grilled pork steaks are served(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the marine wildlife of the region. Included are the epic journeys of sperm whales from one side of the ocean to the other in search of food and a mate, the thriving species that inhabit New Zealand's coast, and the tropical penguins and sea lions supported by the Galapagos Islands(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: The community gathers to mourn the passing of Geraint Ellis, and Mia cannot keep away. That night, she and her friends visit an old ruin with a dark history. Crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams. In English and Welsh(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Cairo
programme.desc: Clive makes his first trip to the Middle East and finds the Egyptian capital is a city of opposites, featuring liberalism and fundamentalism, opulence and poverty and ancient and modern. He visits the pyramids, tries to buy a camel and talks to Omar Sharif about his love for Cairo's traditions of tolerance and cosmopolitanism(n)

programme.title: After the Storm (2016)
programme.desc: Following the death of his father, a private detective struggles to find child support money and reconnect with his son and ex-wife. Comedy drama, starring Hiroshi Abe, Yoko Maki and Satomi Kobayashi. In Japanese(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts an edition first shown on April 13, featuring performances by Cookie Crew with Edwin Starr, Ten City, T'Pau, INXS, Fine Young Cannibals, U2 and BB King, Paul Simpson with Adeva, the Bangles and Yello(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the marine wildlife of the region. Included are the epic journeys of sperm whales from one side of the ocean to the other in search of food and a mate, the thriving species that inhabit New Zealand's coast, and the tropical penguins and sea lions supported by the Galapagos Islands(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Bologna in northern Italy, a city so famous for its food that it's known as the stomach of Italy. He learns how to make the local fresh egg pasta used for tortellini, tagliatelle, ravioli and lasagne and enjoys stuffed rabbit, filled with Parma ham and Parmesan frittata for dinner. Later, Rick drives up into the hills to a cheese dairy where he tastes a 13-year aged Parmesan and enjoys lunch in at a pig farm with restaurant attached, where huge charcoal-grilled pork steaks are served(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: As a boy, frogs were the first animals David Attenborough kept and today he is still just as passionate about them. In this film, he takes viewers into the weird and wonderful world of the amphibian, shedding new light on the charismatic, colourful and frequently bizarre creatures, and examining their anatomy, extraordinary behaviour and ability to survive in some of the most extreme places on the planet(n)

programme.title: The Science of Doctor Who
programme.desc: Brian Cox takes an audience at the Royal Institution of Great Britain's lecture hall on a journey into the universe of sci-fi series Doctor Who, which celebrated its 50th anniversary last month. Brian reveals the science behind the show and explains the physics that allows the Doctor to travel through space and time, and also looks at evidence for the existence of extraterrestrial life. Featuring an appearance by Matt Smith(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Jacky Klein and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit Belfast to take in the Ulster Museum, and assess what are thought to be low-value copies of works by Peter Breughel the Younger. They also head to the Northern Ireland Assembly at Stormont, where they view a controversial image. Said to depict William III and the Pope, the painting was once slashed with a knife, and is now kept in a room away from public view(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores the golden age of piracy during the early 18th century, when Captain Kidd, Blackbeard, Calico Jack and others were in operation on the high seas. He charts the devastating impact they had during an era of colonial expansion and how they became the most wanted outlaws in the world by plundering the vast network of maritime trade, they became the most wanted outlaws in the world(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Diaries
programme.desc: A candid profile of the writer, following him in his home village in Yorkshire, to his community library in Primrose Hill and during a trip to New York. In a series of intimate encounters filmed over the period of a year, he reflects on his modest beginnings and his enduring gratitude to a Welfare State that paid for his education and looked after his parents in their old age(n)

programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining six acts take to the stage for the semi-final, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Wales edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Di Botcher, Charles Dale, Colin Jackson, Shane Williams, Lucie Jones, John Owen-Jones, Ruth Madoc and Sophie Evans(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown and stand-up Darren Harriott take on comedian Susan Calman and Strictly Professional Dancer Johannes Radebe(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Faith and Lev prepare for Luka's surgery and the emergency department rally around to show their support for the family. The team are shocked when Charlie returns to work earlier than expected after Duffy's death, and he is agitated when he realises that things have changed in his absence. Elsewhere, Zsa Zsa interrogates Dylan on the lengths he has gone to for Faith(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Watford v Liverpool at Vicarage Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Crystal Palace at Amex Stadium. The Reds have been in fine from this season, but had to work hard for a 2-0 win in the reverse fixture, which was Nigel Pearson's first match in charge of Watford. Meanwhile the clash between rivals Brighton and Palace took on greater importance than ever, given neither side are safe from the drop. Plus, Bournemouth v Chelsea, Newcastle United v Burnley and West Ham United v Southampton(n)

programme.title: LA Confidential (1997)
programme.desc: Three cops - an ambitious rookie, a hard-boiled veteran and a smooth character more interested in celebrity than justice - investigate a corpse-strewn bloodbath in a diner. However, the ensuing search for the perpetrators reveals a trail of crime and corruption in the police force. Thriller set in 1950s Hollywood, with Russell Crowe, Guy Pearce, Kevin Spacey, James Cromwell, Danny DeVito and an Oscar-winning Kim Basinger(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores Kew Gardens with Alan Titchmarsh, who shares memories from presenting Songs of Praise in the 1980s and '90s and reflects on finding God in nature(n)

programme.title: Puss in Boots: The Three Diablos
programme.desc: Animated short featuring the lovable cat from the hit film series Shrek. Puss in Boots helps three kittens rescue a precious ruby from the evil Whisperer. Featuring the voices of Antonio Banderas, Gilles Marini and Charlotte Newhouse(n)

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway starts a walk in the pretty seaside town of Saundersfoot in Pembrokeshire, currently under-going a multi-million pound seafront regeneration. His nine-mile trek takes him through an area rich in mining history as he heads towards Pendine Sands in neighbouring Carmarthenshire. Derek discovers some fascinating military history as the coast was used as the site for rehearsals for the D-Day landings. The walk leads on to Pendine, with its famous seven-mile stretch of beach famously used for land-speed record attempts(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Host Tudur Owen discovers the truth about later life, referencing the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of sheds, flash cars, and martial arts, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits the Gwent Levels to find out more about a project celebrating the area's fishing and farming heritage, and preserving them for the future. Matt Baker meets a group of fishermen who are still catching salmon the way it's been done for centuries, Ellie Harrison is on the trail of a star species that's made an amazing comeback to the Levels, and Tom Heap is looking at what's being done to ensure those most in need in the countryside can access the healthiest fresh food(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Time-travelling adventures, following the exploits of the Doctor(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where items brought along include an incredibly rare Beatles guitar with an enviable history, which turns out to be one of the most valuable items ever seen on the show. Also featured are royal letters from Queen Victoria's children, a pawned diamond ring that saved a family from the poorhouse, a rare ninth-century gold coin and some valuable Chinese surprises.(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia's relationship comes under increasing strain, while Ted's arrival from New Zealand raises questions. Raff is left speechless when a giraffe turns up at Far Slack Farm and Gillian is riddled with old anxieties when it brings unwarranted attention to the barn. Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present as an audience in Llandudno questions a panel of politicians on the big issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Football Stars in Bad Cars
programme.desc: Fulham team-mates Tom Cairney, Steven Sessegnon and Marcus Bettinelli drive a £2,000 hatchback for the day and play games set by BBC Radio 1's Chris Stark via the car radio. The players then pass the car on to Norwich City after giving it a makeover so that it is even more embarrassing to drive(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Liverpool Council's housing team works on the case of a family whose members left their rented property in an appalling state - and the landlord in shock(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton, Sydenham in south London and Chatham in Kent, then talk to the new owners(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: South African-born Candice Crawford hopes to persuade husband Sean to move to New Zealand permanently after sampling a week in Tauranga. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. The safe proves to be a breakthrough in their investigation into an illegal money lending operation in Dudley in the West Midlands. The loan shark had made hundreds of thousands of pounds by pushing hard-up families further into debt. The enforcers also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al looks forward to the night of the lock-in at the Icon, though as soon as he arrives, his expectations are dampened. Jimmi gets a new cellmate who challenges him to a game of cards, with the winner getting to ask a question of the loser(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Tracking a local conman, Frank and Lu make some unlikely allies when they encounter a pair of deadly gangsters. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives who investigate crime in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple up sticks from Birmingham and spend £500,000 on a country home that allows them to lead the good life while being close enough to Hereford for regular visits to their daughter. While in Herefordshire, Jules visits a family-run blacksmiths that forges commissioned pieces for historic buildings, and takes to the anvil to make a wrought iron accessory for his own home(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sometimes taking big risks can make a difference to the farming industry, and here three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity. However, only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards. Narrated by Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw peruse antique shops in Oxfordshire, Gloucestershire, Warwickshire and Northamptonshire as they head to the final auction in Middle Littleton, Worcestershire. Plus, a visit to Shakespeare's birthplace in Stratford-upon-Avon to find out about a 19th-century, Bard-inspired, American black actor(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As everyone tries to get back to normal following recent events, one person's lies begin to unravel. Meanwhile, Karen inspires Keegan with an idea to cheer up Tiffany, but Keegan's plan ends in disaster(n)

programme.title: Soft Border Patrol
programme.desc: With Brexit over the line Lisa McCoy meets with her political neighbours, just as an Irish politician arrives from Dublin. Niall Sweeney's new in-car technology throws up a few surprises and football legend Jackie Fullerton has some advice for the patrollers. With Karen Hassan, Neil Delamere, Michael Condron, Chris Patrick-Simpson and guest star Jackie Fullerton(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's AJ Pritchard and his brother Curtis choreograph the three queens as they take on an all-singing, all-dancing final challenge. Judges Michelle Visage, Alan Carr, Graham Norton and the eliminated queens all return as the finalists take to the runway one last time before RuPaul crowns the series' ultimate winner(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The participants of Drag Race UK watch the final episode for the very first time, awaiting the big 'Ruveal'. Host RuPaul, judges Michelle Visage, Graham Norton and Alan Carr, the three finalists and the celebrated cast of beautiful queens reflect on the highlights of the series(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Shauna Froydenlund and Vivek Singh, and a special guest faces his or her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for a Swansea couple who want a family space suitable for their two small children. With an ugly concrete outhouse, dangerous broken steps, mismatched levels and wild overgrown corners, the current garden is neither safe not attractive(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Sunday's Carabao Cup final between Manchester City and Aston Villa at Wembley Stadium(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of properties in Carlisle in Cumbria, London's Forest Gate and Walsall in the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge as Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman takes to the road in Wiltshire with a couple of motorbike enthusiasts who, with their £650,000 budget, want to find a home that gives them the perfect balance of community and country life. While in the county, Nicki discovers more about out how conservation efforts to plant hedgerow 'corridors' are creating a safe haven for the UK's endangered tree sparrow population(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages more items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Daniel Heath and Simion Hawtin-Smith helping transforming a floor lamp and a chaise longue(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a toy fire engine, a Japanese Imari bowl that has been smashed to bits, and a unique village sign from Dorset(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are actor and writer Ade Edmondson, comedian and presenter Yung Filly, netballer Serena Guthrie and former rugby player Gareth Thomas on the island of Jersey, Hannibal, Drake and Erasure(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining six acts take to the stage for the semi-final, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Wales edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Di Botcher, Charles Dale, Colin Jackson, Shane Williams, Lucie Jones, John Owen-Jones, Ruth Madoc and Sophie Evans(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown and stand-up Darren Harriott take on comedian Susan Calman and Strictly Professional Dancer Johannes Radebe(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Faith and Lev prepare for Luka's surgery and the emergency department rally around to show their support for the family. The team are shocked when Charlie returns to work earlier than expected after Duffy's death, and he is agitated when he realises that things have changed in his absence. Elsewhere, Zsa Zsa interrogates Dylan on the lengths he has gone to for Faith(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Watford v Liverpool at Vicarage Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Crystal Palace at Amex Stadium. The Reds have been in fine from this season, but had to work hard for a 2-0 win in the reverse fixture, which was Nigel Pearson's first match in charge of Watford. Meanwhile the clash between rivals Brighton and Palace took on greater importance than ever, given neither side are safe from the drop. Plus, Bournemouth v Chelsea, Newcastle United v Burnley and West Ham United v Southampton(n)

programme.title: LA Confidential (1997)
programme.desc: Three cops - an ambitious rookie, a hard-boiled veteran and a smooth character more interested in celebrity than justice - investigate a corpse-strewn bloodbath in a diner. However, the ensuing search for the perpetrators reveals a trail of crime and corruption in the police force. Thriller set in 1950s Hollywood, with Russell Crowe, Guy Pearce, Kevin Spacey, James Cromwell, Danny DeVito and an Oscar-winning Kim Basinger(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Presented by Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Watford v Liverpool at Vicarage Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Crystal Palace at Amex Stadium. The Reds have been in fine from this season, but had to work hard for a 2-0 win in the reverse fixture, which was Nigel Pearson's first match in charge of Watford. Meanwhile the clash between rivals Brighton and Palace took on greater importance than ever, given neither side are safe from the drop. Plus, Bournemouth v Chelsea, Newcastle United v Burnley and West Ham United v Southampton(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around the Province(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at the Deanery CE Academy in Swindon(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sioned Young and her family embark on a trial week living in Perth, but husband Richard is concerned about the practicalities of such a huge change from their lives in North Wales(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of grandmother and granddaughters go head to head in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, under the guidance of John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Presenter Anita Manning gets 'into the groove' and has a go at record collecting(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores Kew Gardens with Alan Titchmarsh, who shares memories from presenting Songs of Praise in the 1980s and '90s and reflects on finding God in nature(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create two gardens inspired by exotic foreign locations, featuring a Moroccan one in Teignmouth and an Asian one in Bitterne(n)

programme.title: Puss in Boots: The Three Diablos
programme.desc: Animated short featuring the lovable cat from the hit film series Shrek. Puss in Boots helps three kittens rescue a precious ruby from the evil Whisperer. Featuring the voices of Antonio Banderas, Gilles Marini and Charlotte Newhouse(n)

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits the Gwent Levels to find out more about a project celebrating the area's fishing and farming heritage, and preserving them for the future. Matt Baker meets a group of fishermen who are still catching salmon the way it's been done for centuries, Ellie Harrison is on the trail of a star species that's made an amazing comeback to the Levels, and Tom Heap is looking at what's being done to ensure those most in need in the countryside can access the healthiest fresh food(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Time-travelling adventures, following the exploits of the Doctor(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where items brought along include an incredibly rare Beatles guitar with an enviable history, which turns out to be one of the most valuable items ever seen on the show. Also featured are royal letters from Queen Victoria's children, a pawned diamond ring that saved a family from the poorhouse, a rare ninth-century gold coin and some valuable Chinese surprises.(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia's relationship comes under increasing strain, while Ted's arrival from New Zealand raises questions. Raff is left speechless when a giraffe turns up at Far Slack Farm and Gillian is riddled with old anxieties when it brings unwarranted attention to the barn. Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, as all four teams looked too boost their chances of playing European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Football Stars in Bad Cars
programme.desc: Fulham team-mates Tom Cairney, Steven Sessegnon and Marcus Bettinelli drive a £2,000 hatchback for the day and play games set by BBC Radio 1's Chris Stark via the car radio. The players then pass the car on to Norwich City after giving it a makeover so that it is even more embarrassing to drive(n)

DCI Vera Stanhope reinvestigates the case, but to find out who really killed Abigail, the detective must uncover the secrets of a close-knit community that is still coming to terms with the incident. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. programme.desc: Martin prepares to face his hearing, but despite having closed the practice, he finds all his patients are trying to make appointments. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi receives an indecent proposal from Ray, Sarah implores Bethany to put her career before Daniel, and Tracy and Peter make sealed bids for Ken's house(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Angus, a member of the Bleakridge Watch, a group of villagers who walk the streets reporting anyone who falls foul of the law, is found dead in his meat freezer. Barnaby and Winter meet Angus's devastated sister and her daughter and learn that Angus was investigating a recent spate of burglaries in the village, but had yet to identify the culprit. Angus' post-mortem reveals strange restraining injuries on his wrists and the plot thickens as Barnaby discovers just how far some people will go for power. Frances Barber guest stars as the leader of the Watch(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Winter and Barnaby investigate when Lower Pampling's star cricketer Leo Henderson is found dead after a match in the grounds, pummelled to death by cricket balls. Barnaby is surprised to see DS Jones undercover as one of Leo's team-mates, and his former colleague reveals he is working on suspected cases of match-fixing in the area, but things take an even more sinister turn when captain Fitz Theara is stabbed through the heart with a cricket stump(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Racial tensions erupt in Hastings when a black GI is suspected of murdering a local woman. The victim, an ex-lover of the suspect and the mother of his baby, was staying at a guest house where Samantha is assistant manager, and had been disowned by her family because of her mixed-race child. As Foyle investigates, he is not convinced the soldier could be capable of such a crime, and soon comes up against the might of the US Army as he tries to crack the case. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Obi Abili and Charlotte Riley(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Crime drama following the personal and professional lives of two detectives working for a Manchester police unit that specialises in murder cases. DC Janet Scott (Lesley Sharp) and DC Rachel Bailey (Suranne Jones) investigate the case of a pregnant woman who was killed in her bedroom. Meanwhile, Rachel's love life takes a turn for the worse and Janet gets a call that reawakens a past tragedy. Also starring Rupert Graves, Amelia Bullmore and Nicholas Gleaves(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present two stories of women trying to bring their families back together. Mother and daughter Val and Marisa Moorhouse from West Yorkshire are united in their search for their missing son and brother Stephen, whom Val had adopted in 1977, when she felt she could not afford to give him the life he deserved. Meanwhile, 37-year-old Sam from Surrey is searching not only for her birth mother, but also her sister(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: An evening of watching telly is ruined when the set breaks down _ but worse is yet to come(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Abi receives an indecent proposal from Ray, Sarah implores Bethany to put her career before Daniel, and Tracy and Peter make sealed bids for Ken's house(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: Comedian Paul O'Grady returns with another Animal Orphans, and having previously visited Africa, he now travels farther east to find out about the rehabilitation of orphaned wild animals in the Malaysian part of Borneo. In the first of two parts, he heads for the orang-utan rehabilitation centre in Sepilok, where he discovers how the animals are being threatened by forest fires, learns about the damage that overfishing is doing to sea turtles' natural habitat, and tries to persuade a gibbon to climb a tree - a skill it needs to learn before it can return to the wild(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Trans-Siberian Adventure
programme.desc: The actress embarks on her third travelogue, having previously traced the Nile from its source and sampled the varied rural, historical and urban cultures of Greece. This time, she sets out to travel 6,400 miles making use of the Trans-Siberian Railway, which is considered the longest railway line in the world. Joanna's journey begins in Hong Kong, where she spent some of her childhood. She takes a bullet train to Beijing, where she meets a woman who claims to have known the last Chinese emperor's favourite concubine, and officially joins the Trans-Siberian track. From there, she travels to the Great Wall, and takes an overnight train into Mongolia via the Gobi desert(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.desc: Samir accuses Emily of calling the immigration officers onto him. Gail's plan for a perfect family Christmas is ruined when Martin drops a bombshell on her(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paul Nicholas guest stars as a Swedish sailor who waits for his ship home in Bernie's funeral parlour - but may not be telling the truth about the contents of the coffin he's escorting. A trio of young joyriders steal Carol's car while she is attending to a patient and decide to experiment with the drugs they find in her medical bag as well as selling them on to interested parties. Rosie and Fergus struggle to get their fledgling relationship off the ground as a series of interruptions plague their dates, while Bellamy starts to wonder whether trying again with Gina is really such a good idea. With Lisa Kay, Vanessa Hehir, Mark Jordon and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Trans-Siberian Adventure
programme.desc: The actress embarks on her third travelogue, having previously traced the Nile from its source and sampled the varied rural, historical and urban cultures of Greece. This time, she sets out to travel 6,400 miles making use of the Trans-Siberian Railway, which is considered the longest railway line in the world. Joanna's journey begins in Hong Kong, where she spent some of her childhood. She takes a bullet train to Beijing, where she meets a woman who claims to have known the last Chinese emperor's favourite concubine, and officially joins the Trans-Siberian track. From there, she travels to the Great Wall, and takes an overnight train into Mongolia via the Gobi desert(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Paul, Nick, Melika and Rhonda pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A teenager dies after taking a seemingly harmless pill, prompting ever-vigilant Quincy to launch a campaign to stop its production and close down a local shop that sells the pills(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck cop with a gambling problem ends up owing money to a gangster - who offers to clear the debt if he kills Crocker. Guest starring Judith Light (Ugly Betty)(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A professional gambler needs to hide a group of people who might be able to help him win at roulette, so Arthur hits on the idea of using Terry's flat. Comedy drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine and featuring a pre-Birds of a Feather Lesley Joseph(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle infiltrate a gang smuggling art treasures and industrial secrets out of the country and selling them on to foreign powers. As the investigation proceeds, the duo discover the illicit operation is being run by a country squire in his stately home. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: Players Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the concluding quarter-final from the Waterfront in Southport, where the first player to win six frames will progress to the semi-finals. Presented by Rishi Persad, with analysis by Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean

programme.title: Live Snooker: Players Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the concluding session of the final from The Waterfront in Southport, as the players compete for the title won by Ronnie O'Sullivan last year, which is the second of three tournaments in the Coral Series. 'The Rocket' was at his brilliant best 12 months ago in Preston to overcome Neil Robertson 10-4, recording a break of 134 in his final frame to ensure he retained the trophy. Presented by Rishi Persad, with analysis by Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates(n)

programme.title: Zero Dark Thirty (2012)
programme.desc: A CIA operative joins a team of intelligence and military operatives in the aftermath of the September 11 attacks. She spends the next 10 years searching for information on the whereabouts of Osama bin Laden, ultimately leading to the final raid on the terrorist leader's hideout. Kathryn Bigelow's fact-based drama, starring Jessica Chastain and Mark Strong(n)
